success_surface: Formula insertion with cross-sheet lookup and error handling for cell-level manipulation tasks
effective_behaviors: Selected openpyxl for formula-level manipulation rather than pandas
Wrote Excel formulas as raw strings with proper syntax
Used dynamic cell references within formula strings for row iteration
Wrapped lookup formulas with error handling to gracefully handle missing data
Saved modified workbook to new output path preserving original file
Systematically iterated through target cell range instead of hardcoding individual cells
Used absolute column references in lookup ranges for robust cross-sheet queries
evidence: Agent used wb['Sheet1'] to access the correct worksheet without listing all sheets
Formula created as f-string: '=IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A{row},Sheet2!$A:$B,2,FALSE),"")' with dynamic row index
For loop iterated range(2, 7) covering all target cells B2:B6
wb.save() wrote to new output path, not modifying source file in-place
IFERROR wrapper ensures empty string output when lookup fails, matching instruction requirement
$A:$B column locking in VLOOKUP ensures range doesn't shift across iterations

memory_items.title: Library Selection for Formulas
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl for direct formula manipulation; pandas loses formula syntax when writing back.
memory_items.generalization: When task requires inserting or modifying Excel formulas (VLOOKUP, SUM, etc.), prefer openpyxl over pandas to preserve formula structure.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Formula Construction
memory_items.lesson: Build formula strings with dynamic references for iterative cell updates.
memory_items.generalization: Use Python f-strings or similar templating to embed changing cell coordinates (like row numbers) within formula strings during loops.

memory_items.title: Error Handling in Lookup Formulas
memory_items.lesson: Wrap lookup functions with error handlers when referenced data may be incomplete.
memory_items.generalization: Add IFERROR or equivalent wrappers around VLOOKUP/HLOOKUP/MATCH formulas to return safe defaults (empty string, zero) when lookups fail.

memory_items.title: Cross-Sheet Reference Syntax
memory_items.lesson: Reference other worksheets using 'SheetName!Range' notation in formula strings.
memory_items.generalization: When formulas span multiple sheets, include the sheet name before the cell/range reference using exclamation notation.

memory_items.title: Iterative Range Processing
memory_items.lesson: Loop through target cell ranges programmatically rather than manually updating each cell.
memory_items.generalization: When filling multiple adjacent cells with similar logic, iterate over row/column indices and construct addresses dynamically.

edits.content:
## 6. Formula Best Practices
- **Dynamic References**: When iterating cells, use Python string formatting (f-strings) to insert changing coordinates into formula templates.
- **Error Safety**: Wrap lookup operations (VLOOKUP, XLOOKUP, MATCH) with IFERROR or equivalents when source data may have gaps; specify fallback values (empty string, 0) per task requirements.
- **Cross-Sheet Syntax**: Use 'SheetName!Range' notation for formulas referencing other worksheets; anchor lookup ranges with absolute references ($A:$B) to prevent offset errors during iteration.
- **Range Iteration**: Loop through target cell ranges programmatically rather than hardcoding individual cell updates for scalability.
